↩️ Reply to OSINT OSINTdefender Potential Russian-backed false flag currently underway in Hungary. 💥 What we’ve all feared is happening: Hungarian Russia expert András Rácz wrote three days ago about a potential Russia-backed false flag attack in Serbia targeting the gas pipeline to Hungary. The same information had already reached multiple journalists, including myself, weeks earlier, from sources connected to Hungarian government circles. Now Viktor Orbán has announced that Serbian president Aleksandar Vučić informed him about “explosives of devastating power” found at the gas pipeline connecting the two countries. Orbán and his propaganda machine are already amplifying the news everywhere, with the prime minister convening his security cabinet. It remains unclear what measures the government might take using this alleged false flag operation as a pretext. But if the second part of the information we received also proves true, Orbán could declare a state of emergency, significantly affecting the election campaign—which he is currently losing—and potentially disrupting the organization of the April 12 election. The opposition Tisza Party has been widening its lead to 15–20 points, if not more. Orbán accuses them of being "Ukrainian agents" for months. His propaganda would very soon link the Serbian false-flag both to Ukraine and the Tisza Party, I have no doubts about that. I encourage all foreign reporters covering the Hungarian election to pay close attention and not fall for the government’s propaganda or the narratives pushed by its pundits on the Orbán government payroll, including here on X. The situation could soon be very serious. - Szabolcs Panyi tweet

Nuno Felix RT @ZelenskyyUa : Talks with the President of Syria, Ahmed al-Sharaa @AH_AlSharaa . We agreed to work together to bring greater security and more opportunities for development to our societies. We discussed the situation in the region and the prospects for changing it for the better. We also went over the circumstances of Russia’s war against Ukraine – I am grateful for the support. There is strong interest in exchanging military and security experience. I appreciate the words of respect for our people. We also touched on Ukraine’s role as a reliable supplier of food products and discussed joint opportunities to strengthen food security across the region. We understand very well the energy and infrastructure challenges Syria is currently facing. We are ready to work together to expand opportunities for both our countries and their people. tweet

Michael A. Horowitz Ukraine and Syria reportedly agreed to work together to strengthen food security in the region and develop bilateral cooperation. They also discussed Russia's war against Ukraine, the exchange of military and security expertise, and Syria's energy and infrastructure challenges. Ukrainian President Zelenskyy is visiting #Syria today https://twitter.com/michaelh992/status/2040788615752536487/photo/1 - Michael A. Horowitz tweet

Nuno Felix Black Sea powers meet Syrian Government in Damascus Ukrainian President Zelensky and Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan arrive in Damascus to meet Syrian President Ahmad al-Sharaa today. - AlexandruC4 tweet

↩️ Reply to OSINT Nuno Felix RT @alex98almeida : está apresentado These fucking Greeks. First the shipping oligarchs profit from moving Russian oil and selling decrepit tankers to Russians for their dark fleet - now this: The former Greek Finance Minister, Yanis Varoufakis, came to Moscow to speak at the Russian T-Bank forum for investors and traders. The economist took the stage to the track with his name, which went viral among Zoomers in late March. The song was written with a neural network by Moscow DJ Sasha Melior, and in March, it went viral in clubs where Moscow's Zoomers gather. Accusations: “Putin apologist” & “West-splaining” Since 2022, critics (especially in Eastern Europe) have accused him of: •Minimising Ukrainian agency •Overemphasising Western culpability •Promoting “peace realism” that may benefit Russia Varoufakis himself acknowledges these accusations: •He has been labelled a “Putin apologist” and “conspiracy theorist” for his views. And rightly so. Securing his place on the fence of “neutrality” he blags that he is anti-war and anti-imperialist across the board, not pro-Russian. He also insists criticism of NATO or sanctions doesn’t mean support for Moscow. He banked the money for his appearance before a pro-Russian, pro-genocide crown of Russians as a financial rock star from #Greece. Will @vonderleyen act? I doubt it. - Beefeater tweet

🇺🇸 ⚔️ 🇮🇷 This is a map of Iran superimposed over the United States, since some people seem to struggle with grasping just how large the country is. The other image shows Iran’s terrain. Hopefully this adds a bit of perspective for those saying “muh… Iran can’t even cover their airspace.” Iran is roughly the size of Alaska, about 2.5 times the size of Texas, and spans nearly one-sixth of the U.S. land area. And for our American friends: That’s about ~68.7 million Walmart Supercenters, ~150 trillion Big Macs, or roughly ~300 billion F-150s parked side by side (no spacing) 🔴 @DDGeopolitics
